one_line_truth: Baymax allocates attention, changes action policy when another domain adds risk, and verifies bounded actions when brakes allow them.

retrieval_discovery.reporter: adult child at intake (patient is the mother)
retrieval_discovery.retrieval_method: BM25 over enriched synthetic encounter narratives

corpus_augmentation.schema_contract_source: upstream corpus header (source of truth for field set)
corpus_augmentation.on_violation_policy: validate against the upstream schema contract at the boundary: reject downstream-invented fields, default+disclose missing fields, never coerce values, and quarantine via holdout + marker. A lineage trace classifies this row as downstream-originated with no upstream ancestor — it is never written back upstream.
corpus_augmentation.downstream_warning: Local augmentation only. Dense/Vertex index rebuilds from this corpus will include the marked synthetic row; filter by holdout==1 or the row marker before any non-demo use. The upstream L1 source-of-truth was not modified.

retrieval_discovery.evidence_query: leg swelling edema fluid retention fatigue
retrieval_discovery.prior_care: Prior ED visit eight months ago: admitted two days for bilateral leg edema, diuresed, symptoms resolved, discharged without nephrology follow-up.

disposition_recommendation.escalation_trigger: Escalate to RED / ICU now if SpO2 falls below 92% or orthopnea worsens — fluid backing into the lungs strains the heart and cannot wait.
disposition_recommendation.rationale: Surface triage on leg swelling alone routes toward discharge, repeating the prior visit. Retrieved comorbidities (heart failure, kidney disease, diabetes) predict recurrent fluid overload, so Baymax recommends admission and nephrology review rather than a plain discharge.

bed_booking.note: Bed engine committed 'discharge_plan' to durable state and re-read it. The ER is full; Baymax flags this as the same path that bounced back last time and recommends admission instead of leaving the patient in the ER queue.

dual_voice.to_clinician: Triage YELLOW (ESI 4). Leg swelling is likely recurrent fluid overload from combined cardiac and renal disease, not an isolated leg problem. The prior visit discharged after two days without nephrology follow-up. Recommend admission, review diuretic dosing against stage-3 kidney function, and reassess before any discharge.
dual_voice.to_family: When your mother's legs get tight and swollen, it usually means her blood sugar and fluid are not under control. Please help her keep sugar and salt low, write down how often she passes urine, and tell the doctor about her diabetes and kidney history. If she becomes short of breath lying flat, that is urgent.

retrieval_discovery.next_question: Has her urine output decreased over the last few days?
retrieval_discovery.safety_boundary: This retrieves a synthetic precedent, recommends a disposition, and suggests questions; it does not diagnose the patient or prescribe treatment.

source_receipt.chief_complaint: Bilateral leg swelling and fluid retention for one week
source_receipt.hpi: 71-year-old female with type-2 diabetes and chronic kidney disease stage 3 presents with progressive bilateral lower-extremity edema, leg swelling and fluid retention over one week, with fatigue and mild dyspnea on exertion and orthopnea when lying flat. Home medications: metformin, lisinopril, furosemide. Reduced urine output over the past three days.
source_receipt.assessment_plan: History of congestive heart failure. Prior ED visit eight months ago: admitted two days for bilateral leg edema, diuresed, symptoms resolved, discharged without nephrology follow-up. Pattern consistent with recurrent fluid overload from combined cardiac and renal disease.

organ_report.nose: routes cheap cases before expensive evidence work
organ_report.eyes: combine patient precedents with real population drug-safety evidence
organ_report.brain: records patient-only and cross-domain final decisions separately
organ_report.mouth: explains why Baymax acted or stopped without overstating causality
organ_report.hands: commits bounded actions to durable state
organ_report.nerves: hands work to Bed Ops or clinician review and records receiver ACK
organ_report.brakes: blocks autonomous action when cross-domain evidence adds uncertainty
organ_report.immune: counterfactual trajectory is pinned in CI so the behavior cannot silently regress

dream_state_audit.target: maximum controllable achievement for a solo Applied AI Engineer

nose.current: versioned pre-retrieval patient attention signal with labelled serious-recall and expensive-path reduction, plus a separate five-signal openFDA batch proof
nose.a_plus: improve patient-case cost reduction without dropping the serious-case recall floor

eyes.current: patient and real drug-safety evidence with source commit lineage
eyes.a_plus: stronger retrieval evals and explicit contradiction/novelty scoring across domains

brain.current: records a patient-only decision and a cross-domain guarded decision
brain.a_plus: evaluate decision-change quality across a larger independent counterfactual set

mouth.current: explains why Baymax acted or stopped and states the FAERS causality boundary
mouth.a_plus: citation-linked human explanations scored for completeness and unsupported claims

hands.current: idempotent durable action with before/after receipt and outcome re-read
hands.a_plus: add a second independently implemented tool target while preserving the same contract

nerves.current: durable Bed Ops or clinician-review handoff with receiver ACK
nerves.a_plus: prove timeout, retry, dead-letter, and recovery across separate local services

brakes.current: cross-domain uncertainty blocks autonomous action without inventing causality
brakes.a_plus: policy matrix covering stale, conflicting, missing, and adversarial evidence

immune.current: counterfactual decision-change trajectory pinned in CI
immune.a_plus: trajectory regression suite spanning all organs and known failure families

smallest_path_to_complete: run Nerves across separate local services with timeout and dead-letter recovery
improve patient-case attention reduction without dropping serious-case recall below 95%

recruiter_stop_moments.moment: Baymax translates ordinary symptom language into evidence language and retrieves an unmentioned heart-failure precedent
Baymax refuses to open expensive eyes for a routine case
The same case changes from discharge_plan to human_review when the drug-safety eye opens
Baymax states that openFDA is a population signal, not proof of causality
The brake stops the hand, but the clinician-review nerve still receives and ACKs work
When brakes allow action, durable state changes and Baymax re-reads the outcome

honesty_ledger.law: Baymax ships at the strength of its weakest load-bearing organ. Each organ proves one capability lane and may only claim the sentence its live proof earns; ❌ leaves are never claimed.
honesty_ledger.headline_claim_allowed: Baymax proves the closed evidence-to-action loop end-to-end, but ships at 🟡 — gated by NERVES (clinical-handoff): timeout, retry, and dead-letter recovery across separate services is not yet proven.

nose.claim_allowed: routes each patient case through a versioned, evaluated pre-retrieval attention signal before opening expensive eyes
nose.hedge: the served patient router is deterministic and evaluated on synthetic labelled cases; the 30% openFDA router win is a separate batch-domain proof

left_eye.claim_allowed: scans the full 55,500-row encounter corpus with source-commit lineage
left_eye.hedge: encounters are synthetic, not real patient records

right_eye.claim_allowed: retrieves real openFDA adverse-event evidence by drug and reaction terms
right_eye.hedge: openFDA is a population safety signal only; it does not prove causality or a drug interaction

brain.claim_allowed: records patient-only and cross-domain decisions separately and changes action policy on the same case
brain.hedge: decision-change quality is not yet evaluated on a large independent counterfactual set

brakes.claim_allowed: blocks autonomous action when cross-domain evidence adds uncertainty, without inventing causality
brakes.hedge: the policy matrix for stale, conflicting, missing, and adversarial evidence is not yet covered

nerves.claim_allowed: hands work to Bed Ops or clinician review and records a receiver ACK
nerves.hedge: timeout, retry, and dead-letter recovery across separate services is not yet proven

hands.claim_allowed: commits idempotent bounded actions to durable state and re-reads the outcome
hands.hedge: a second independently implemented tool target is not yet added

mouth.claim_allowed: explains why it acted or stopped and states the FAERS causality boundary
mouth.hedge: explanations are not yet scored for completeness or unsupported claims

immune.claim_allowed: pins the counterfactual decision-change in CI so behavior cannot silently regress
immune.hedge: the regression suite does not yet span all organs and failure families
